stem: A proposal enters the grant application pipeline. The probability of passing departmental review is between 66% and 77% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing external peer review is 82%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing panel discussion is 93%. What is the overall probability that the proposal is funded?

ground_truth.answer: P(funded) ranges from 0.503 to 0.587 as the departmental review pass rate varies from 66% to 77%. Point estimate: 0.549.

solution_steps: The proposal must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the departmental review stage creates a 8.4 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the departmental review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
